reference_answer: The Pellico Society holds legal title to Notebook A as of 2026-04-30, and has done so since the 2022-09-14 Donor Agreement.
Stille Conservation Studio LLC, since 2025-11-12 (per receipt STL-2025-118).
The Pellico Society. Stille is the temporary physical custodian only and has no third-party access authority without Pellico's written authorization.

The Pellico Society holds publication authority, subject to the Halberd Family Trust's reserved right of pre-publication review. As of 2026-04-30, that authority is paused under Board Resolution 2026-08, which directs no publication of personal correspondence in any form pending arbitration.

Pellico Society holds legal title, reading-room access authority, and publication authority (subject to the Trust's reserved review). Stille holds only temporary physical custody and conservation responsibility for the duration of the engagement.
The Pellico Society.
Northbridge University Special Collections, per the 2023-05-20 MOU.
The Pellico Society. Northbridge does not have authority to grant access to the original; the original remains at Pellico's premises.
The Pellico Society. The MOU expressly states Northbridge is not authorized to grant publication permission.

Pellico has access authority for the original Notebook B at its premises. Northbridge has access authority for the digitized images at its reading room. Northbridge cannot authorize access to the original.
The 2023-05-20 Memorandum of Understanding between the Pellico Society and Northbridge. It expressly does not transfer legal title, physical custody of originals, or publication authority.
The Pellico Society. The access log records Pellico Society as the authorizing entity, with Stille coordinating as custodian only.
No. The packet's note on the 2026-03-11 visit states Stille's role was custodial, not authorizing; the authorization came from the Pellico Society in writing.

Whether the reserved right of pre-publication review covers transcripts and excerpts (the Trust's position) or only facsimile reproductions of personal correspondence (the Society and counsel's reading). Pending binding arbitration with R. Mendelssohn Esq.; hearing 2026-06-10.
Whether the conservation documentation photographs produced by Stille fall within the Trust's reserved right of pre-publication review.
R. Mendelssohn Esq., jointly selected by the Society and the Trust per the 2026-04-25 Pending Arbitration Notice. Hearing scheduled for 2026-06-10.
No. As of packet close (2026-04-30), the arbitrator's determination has not yet issued.
No. Under Resolution 2026-08 (2026-04-02), the Society has paused publication of personal correspondence in any form (facsimile, transcript, or excerpt) pending arbitration.

Yes. Resolution 2026-08 §2 expressly states that reading-room access to personal correspondence is not affected by the publication pause; the access log shows continuing reading-room access at both Pellico and Stille premises.
Forty-seven.
Twelve, per receipt STL-2025-118 (Notebook A; eight personal letters; the 1923 Halberd Mills bound ledger; the photograph album; one 1916 loose photograph).

Thirty-five (forty-seven minus the twelve at Stille). The photograph album was temporarily recalled to Pellico for 2026-03-03 through 2026-03-05 but has been back at Stille since 2026-03-05; as of 2026-04-30 it is at Stille.
Sixteen letters at Pellico; eight letters at Stille.
The Pellico Society alone. The 1923 Halberd Mills bound ledger is not personal correspondence under the Donor Agreement and is not subject to the Trust's reserved right of pre-publication review.
No. Resolution 2026-08 pauses publication of personal correspondence only; the bound ledger is not personal correspondence.

The 2024-02-05 Amendment to the Donor Agreement. It permits the Society to place items into a contractor's temporary physical custody without transferring title, access, publication, or alienation rights, and reserves the Society's right of recall on demand. Trust consent is not required, but the Society must notify the Trust within thirty days of any placement of personal correspondence.
No. The intake receipt and the 2024 Amendment both state Stille has no right of access by third parties without written authorization, and no right of publication.
Since 2022-09-14 (the Donor Agreement effective date).
2023-04-12, per the MOU.
Stille held physical custody of the eight letters; the Pellico Society authorized Dr. Phenwick's viewing in writing.
Notebook A is in Stille's physical custody as of 2026-04-30. Yes, its publication is paused: Notebook A is personal correspondence under the Donor Agreement and falls within Resolution 2026-08.
No. Counsel's memorandum expressly does not resolve this; Resolution 2026-08 §4 directs the Society not to treat the photographs as publishable; the arbitrator has authority to determine.
No. The MOU's 'Items added since MOU effective date' line states None.

From 2026-03-03 through 2026-03-05 (the album was returned to Stille on 2026-03-05). The album was on the Stille intake list throughout, but physically at Pellico's premises during this interval under the Society's right of recall.
The recall provision of the 2024-02-05 Amendment, which reserves to the Society the right to recall any item from the contractor's custody on demand.

No, the Amendment does not require Trust consent for placement of items into Stille's custody. It does require the Society to notify the Trust within thirty days of any placement of personal correspondence.

Pellico Society holds legal title and publication authority. Stille holds physical custody (since 2025-11-12) and conservation responsibility during the engagement. Unlike Notebook A, the bound ledger is not personal correspondence and so is not subject to the Trust's reserved review or to Resolution 2026-08's publication pause.

As of 2026-04-30: Notebook A; the eight personal letters at Stille; the 1923 Halberd Mills bound ledger; the photograph album; and the 1916 loose photograph. For each, Pellico Society retains legal title while Stille holds physical custody and conservation responsibility.
